cards.meaning: situation; state of affairs (esp. political, military, or economic)
cards.example: 中东局势又紧张起来了。
cards.examplePinyin: zhōng dōng jú shì yòu jǐn zhāng qǐ lái le
cards.exampleMeaning: The situation in the Middle East has grown tense again.
cards.note: 书面语/formal — the big political/military/economic picture. Contrast 情况 (qíngkuàng), colloquial and usable for any small matter (我的情况有点特殊, 'my circumstances are a bit special'), and 形势 (xíngshì), which stresses the direction/trend rather than the current configuration. Finance-desk register: 局势 for a geopolitical whole.

cards.meaning: hard to avoid; inevitably; bound to
cards.example: 海峡一关闭，油价上涨难免。
cards.examplePinyin: hǎi xiá yī guān bì, yóu jià shàng zhǎng nán miǎn
cards.exampleMeaning: Once the strait closes, a rise in oil prices is unavoidable.
cards.note: HSK 4 connective-adverb with a slightly resigned tone; usual pattern 难免会… ('bound to…'), e.g. 市场难免会波动 ('the market is bound to be volatile'). Near-synonym contrast: 必然 (bìrán) is logical inevitability — harder, more absolute — while 难免 is 'hard to avoid in the nature of things.' Register: 书面语-leaning but common in speech.
cards.tiesTo: war premium returning to oil once Hormuz shuts

cards.meaning: the ravaging or onslaught of a destructive force — a storm, a crisis
cards.example: Sa hilagang Luzon, patuloy ang pananalasa ng habagat.
cards.exampleMeaning: In northern Luzon, the ravaging of the monsoon continues.
cards.note: Native-speaker nuance: pananalasa is the action — the onslaught, the destroying as it happens — while pinsala is the result, what got damaged ('ang pananalasa ng bagyo ay nag-iwan ng malaking pinsala'). Used figuratively too: ang pananalasa ng implasyon, 'the ravaging of inflation.'
cards.tiesTo: Super Typhoon Inday / habagat over Luzon

cards.meaning: livelihood; the whole economic means by which one lives
cards.example: Ang tumataas na presyo ay bumabawas sa kabuhayan ng maraming pamilya.
cards.exampleMeaning: Rising prices eat into the livelihood of many families.
cards.note: Native-speaker nuance: broader than hanapbuhay (a job/occupation — the work you seek to earn from). Kabuhayan is the whole economic life — income, means of support, including the remittances from abroad that sustain millions of Filipino households. Register: everyday but weighty, the word for a family's economic footing.
cards.tiesTo: record minimum-wage hike, cost of living, remittances
